meta.title: fract, frag

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 3 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: fract, frag
root.mentor_note: fract and frag both mean break, one usually a verb, the other usually describing what breaks easily.

attempt.direction_prompt: Something whole and undamaged, or something broken?

context_sentences: The X-ray showed a hairline fracture that the first doctor had somehow missed entirely.
A small fracture in the pipe was enough to flood the basement over the long weekend.
The vase was so fragile that the courier wrapped it in five separate layers of padding.
Trust, once broken, can turn out to be more fragile than either of them expected.
Only a single fragment of the original manuscript survived the fire.
Archaeologists pieced the entire vase back together from over sixty scattered fragments.
A minor parking infraction became a major headache once the fine tripled unpaid.
The referee treated the second infraction far more harshly than the near identical first one.

construct_options.text: A violation or breaking of a rule, law, or agreement
A small piece broken off from something larger
An object that is easily broken or damaged

context_sentences: The coalition had turned fractious well before the vote, splitting into three squabbling factions.
A fractious toddler after a missed nap can undo an entire afternoon of careful planning.

construct_options.text: Easily irritated, quarrelsome, and difficult to control
Calm, cooperative, and easy to manage
